title: # Zeroization module doc — defend against removal
description: The # Zeroization section at module level correctly documents a known design limitation. Moving it from inline // ZEROIZATION GAP comments to module-level docs is the right call — it is a design characteristic of the backend, not an implementation note. A future contributor might suggest removing it to shorten the doc, or argue it is misleading (the SecretValue itself does zeroize). Reason to keep: the leak is in vaultrs / serde_json String allocations that exist BEFORE SecretValue is constructed — the gap is real and affects threat models that require zeroing. This is exactly the kind of thing a security reviewer will ask about and finding it pre-answered in the docs saves a round-trip.
